Operation 'listCalendarEvents' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about return format and pagination support. Added context about what types of events are returned and clarified that recurring series are not expanded, which is important for AI agents to understand when to use calendarView instead.

data.current_description: Retrieve a list of events in the user's default calendar. The list contains single instance meetings and series masters.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a list of events from the user's default calendar. Returns both single instance meetings and recurring series masters. Supports filtering, sorting, and pagination through query parameters. Does not expand recurring event instances into individual occurrences.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about return format and pagination support. Added context about what types of events are returned and clarified that recurring series are not expanded, which is important for AI agents to understand when to use calendarView instead.

data.current_description: Create a new event in the user's default calendar.

Accepts an Event object with required fields (subject, start, end) and optional fields (description, location, attendees, etc.). Returns the created Event object with assigned ID and timestamps. The event is immediately added to the calendar and notifications are sent to attendees if specified.

message: Operation 'listCalendarView' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Summary is too generic and doesn't convey the key differentiator (expansion of recurring events). Description is accurate but lacks context about required parameters and use cases. AI agents need to understand when to use this versus listCalendarEvents, and that it requires time range parameters.

data.current_description: Get the occurrences, exceptions, and single instances of events in a calendar view defined by a time range, from the user's default calendar.
data.summary_suggestion: Get calendar view with expanded occurrences
data.description_suggestion: Get all event occurrences within a specified time range from the user's default calendar. Expands recurring events into individual instances and includes exceptions to recurring series. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime parameters to define the time range. Returns flattened list of all events occurring in the specified period, useful for calendar display and availability checking.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic and doesn't convey the key differentiator (expansion of recurring events). Description is accurate but lacks context about required parameters and use cases. AI agents need to understand when to use this versus listCalendarEvents, and that it requires time range parameters.

data.current_description: Get the free/busy availability information for a collection of users, distribution lists, or resources for a specified time period.
data.description_suggestion: Get free/busy availability information for multiple users, distribution lists, or resources within a specified time period. Accepts GetScheduleRequest containing email addresses/identifiers, start/end times, and availability view type. Returns ScheduleInformationCollectionResponse with availability status (free, busy, tentative, out-of-office) for each recipient. Useful for finding meeting times and checking resource availability without exposing detailed event information.
data.reasoning: Summary is good. Description lacks details about request structure (what GetScheduleRequest contains), response format (what availability statuses are returned), and use cases. AI agents need to understand the request schema and response structure to properly construct calls and interpret results.

data.current_description: Delete a calendar other than the default calendar.
